commands.review_summary:
The PR removes Slack and Microsoft Teams inbound body previews from queued system-event labels and updates focused regressions to assert the full body remains in BodyForAgent.

PR surface: Source 0, Tests +7. Total +7 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. at source level: current main and v2026.6.8 still build Slack and Teams system events from `${inboundLabel}: ${preview}` while the full body is separately delivered. I did not run a live Slack or Microsoft Teams tenant scenario in this read-only review.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blocker is contributor or maintainer real behavior proof for live Slack or Microsoft Teams; there is no narrow code repair for ClawSweeper to queue from this review.

target.title: fix(channels): stop duplicating inbound previews in system events
target.body:
## Summary

- Fixes a prompt-context duplication bug where Slack and Microsoft Teams inbound handlers copied a 160-character user-message preview into queued `System:` events even though the full user body is already delivered through the normal inbound context.
- This matters now because #94549 shows the issue still reproduces on recent OpenClaw releases: long Slack/Teams messages can appear truncated in model-visible system-event text, making the model think the user message was cut off.
- The intended outcome is notification-only system events such as `Slack DM from Alice` / `Teams message in channel from Member`, while the full message remains available through `BodyForAgent` and the existing channel history/body paths.
- Out of scope: changing system-event formatting globally, changing channel history behavior, changing reaction/thread-parent system events, or adding live Slack/Teams transport behavior.
- Success looks like Slack and Teams no longer duplicate inbound body text in system-event labels, with focused regression coverage proving the body still reaches the agent path.
- Reviewers should focus on the two enqueue call sites and the updated regression assertions in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts` and `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts`.

## Linked context

Closes #94549

Related #67503
Related #67761
Related #93966
Related #12850
Related #88261

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

No. I selected this from the public issue queue after ClawSweeper marked it source-reproducible, queueable, and clear in fix shape.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Slack and Microsoft Teams inbound message handlers were enqueueing user-authored body previews into model-visible system events, causing duplicate and sometimes truncated copies of the user's message to appear before the full body.
- Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `codex/fix-94549-inbound-system-preview`, Node v22.22.0.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts
git diff --check
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

- Evidence after fix:

```text
[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-mattermost.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  10 passed (10)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-msteams.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  18 passed (18)

[test] starting test/vitest/vitest.extension-slack.config.ts
Test Files  1 passed (1)
Tests  85 passed (85)

[test] passed 3 Vitest shards in 44.73s

Checking formatting...
All matched files use the correct format.

autoreview clean: no accepted/actionable findings reported
overall: patch is correct (0.82)
```

- Observed result after fix: The Slack regression now expects `enqueueSystemEvent("Slack DM from Alice", ...)` while separately asserting the full body still appears in `BodyForAgent`. The Teams regressions now find `Teams message in channel from Member` system events and assert the inbound body text is absent from the system-event label while still present in the dispatched `BodyForAgent`.
- What was not tested: Live Slack or Microsoft Teams delivery against real workspace/tenant credentials.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: The focused proof exercises the production Slack `prepareSlackMessage` and Microsoft Teams `createMSTeamsMessageHandler` paths through OpenClaw's extension test harness with mocked Slack/Teams transport and runtime boundaries. It proves the prompt-context construction behavior under this patch, but it is not a live provider/channel end-to-end run.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Before this change, Slack enqueued `${inboundLabel}: ${preview}` in `extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts`, and Teams enqueued the same `${inboundLabel}: ${preview}` shape in `extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.ts`. Reverting those two production-line changes makes the updated label-only regression assertions fail because the system-event text again contains the user body preview.

## Tests and validation

Commands run:

```bash
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.inbound-system-event.test.ts
git diff --check
node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts
node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.extensions.json extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/prepare.test.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.ts extensions/msteams/src/monitor-handler/message-handler.authz.test.ts
.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local
```

Regression coverage added or updated:

- Updated Slack prepare coverage to assert the queued inbound system event does not include body text while `BodyForAgent` retains the full message.
- Updated Teams authz coverage for both skipped and dispatched channel messages to assert the primary system event remains owner-neutral and body-free, while dispatched messages still carry the full `BodyForAgent`.
- Ran Mattermost inbound system-event tests as sibling proof that the already-correct regular-post behavior remains green.

What failed before this fix:

- The updated Slack and Teams label-only assertions fail on the old code path because the queued system-event text includes `: ${preview}` from the user body.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

Yes. Model-visible channel context changes for Slack and Microsoft Teams inbound messages: duplicate/truncated body previews are removed from `System:` event text.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

Prompt/context semantics for Slack and Microsoft Teams inbound turns.

How is that risk mitigated?

The change is limited to the system-event label string. Session keys, context keys, routing, history recording, debug previews, and the normal full-body delivery path are preserved. Focused regression tests cover both removed duplication and retained `BodyForAgent` content.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Awaiting maintainer review and CI after PR creation.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI has not run yet. Live Slack/Teams tenant proof was not run locally; the current proof is focused source-checkout proof through production handler paths with mocked transport/runtime boundaries.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

Fresh local autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.

AI-assisted (Codex). Human-run local validation is recorded above.
target.author: hugenshen
